---
title: "Sketched and AR-aware models"
subtitle: "Low-rank sketch and AR whitening through the typed engine seam."
jupyter: python3
execute:
  freeze: auto
---

Sketching and AR whitening are *engine* choices, not separate APIs. You
keep the same typed spec and dataset and select the engine or AR config
on `fmri_lm` — the result is still a typed `FmriLm`.

## Why Sketch?

For large fMRI matrices, a randomized sketch reduces the problem size
before solving the GLM. Pass `SketchEngineOptions` as the engine; compare
its betas to the default fit on the same typed spec.

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import fmrimod as fm
from fmrimod.spec import hrf
from fmrimod.glm.engine import SketchEngineOptions
from fmrimod.model.config import FmriLmConfig, AROptions

rng = np.random.default_rng(14)
events = pd.DataFrame({
    "onset": np.arange(0, 21 * 8, 8),
    "condition": ["face", "scene", "tool"] * 7,
    "duration": 2.0,
})
spec = hrf("condition", basis="spmg1")

# Realize the design from the typed spec, then simulate signal on it.
scaffold = fm.fmri_dataset(np.zeros((180, 40)), tr=2.0, events=events)
X = fm.fmri_lm(spec, scaffold).model.design_matrix_array(run=0)
beta = rng.normal(size=(X.shape[1], 40))
Y = X @ beta + rng.normal(scale=0.3, size=(180, 40))
dataset = fm.fmri_dataset(Y, tr=2.0, events=events)

full = fm.fmri_lm(spec, dataset)
sketched = fm.fmri_lm(spec, dataset, engine=SketchEngineOptions(sketch_ratio=0.6, seed=1))

full.betas.shape, sketched.betas.shape
```

## Compare Estimates

```{python}
np.corrcoef(full.betas.ravel(), sketched.betas.ravel())[0, 1].round(4)
```

The sketched solve recovers the full-rank estimates to within rounding on
this design — the speed/accuracy trade is a single typed engine argument.

## AR-Aware Fitting

Temporal autocorrelation is handled by the AR config, not by hand-rolling
residual ACFs. Fit with `AROptions(struct="ar1")` and read the estimated
AR coefficients straight off the typed result.

```{python}
ar_fit = fm.fmri_lm(
    spec, dataset, config=FmriLmConfig(ar=AROptions(struct="ar1"))
)
np.ravel(ar_fit.ar_params).round(3), ar_fit.residual_df
```

`ar_params` is near zero here because the simulated noise is white;
on real data it quantifies the autocorrelation the whitening removed.

## AR Noise Fixture

When you *do* want a raw autocorrelation read — e.g. validating a noise
model — `simulate_noise_vector` and `acorr_diagnostics` operate on a
plain signal. An AR(0.5) process shows the expected decaying ACF.

```{python}
ar_noise = fm.simulate_noise_vector(
    180, tr=2.0, ar=[0.5], ma=[],
    drift_amplitude=0.0, physio=False, seed=9,
)
fm.acorr_diagnostics(ar_noise, max_lag=3)["acf"].round(3)
```

## Next

- [Linear model](linear-model.qmd)
- [Simulation](simulation.qmd)
